pitch.title: Voidwalkers
pitch.logline: When a death row inmate's mind is uploaded to a deep-space mining drone, he discovers an alien anomaly that could clear his name if he can hijack the drone and navigate it back to Earth before his physical body is executed.
pitch.core_theme: The resilience of the human spirit and the search for redemption.
pitch.synopsis: Act I: In 2145, disgraced pilot Jax is framed for treason and sentenced to 'The Rig,' a program where inmates' minds pilot expendable drones in hazardous asteroid fields. Act II: During a routine extraction, Jax's drone uncovers a derelict ship containing evidence of the conspiracy that framed him. To retrieve it, he must break his drone's programming tethers, battling both the harsh environment of space and the warden's digital countermeasures. Act III: Jax successfully transfers the data to a rogue satellite, proving his innocence seconds before his execution, bringing down the corrupt regime and winning his freedom.

stage2_outline.title: Voidwalkers
stage2_outline.logline: When a death row inmate's mind is uploaded to a deep-space mining drone, he discovers an alien anomaly that could clear his name if he can hijack the drone and navigate it back to Earth before his physical body is executed.

beats.description: Jax sits in a sterile cell awaiting his execution date, staring at a photo of the family he lost.
Guards strap Jax into the neural-link chair of The Rig, projecting his consciousness into a clunky mining drone millions of miles away to work off his debt to society.
During a brutal asteroid mining shift, Jax's drone sensors detect a strange alien anomaly that conceals a derelict ship--the exact vessel he was framed for destroying.
Jax hesitates to deviate from his assigned mining path, knowing the Warden monitors all drone telemetry and punishes disobedience with agonizing neural shocks.
Desperate for redemption, Jax overrides his drone's primary directive and thrusts into the uncharted sector, officially going rogue to investigate the anomaly.
Jax interacts with another inmate drone, a cynical hacker named Rix, who agrees to help mask Jax's coordinates in exchange for a cut of potential salvage.
Jax navigates his bulky drone through a dense, hazardous asteroid field, dodging debris while Rix feeds him code to bypass the Warden's tracking algorithms.
Jax breaches the derelict ship and extracts the black box data proving a high-ranking official framed him, but the extraction triggers a massive power surge that alerts the Warden.
The Warden realizes Jax has uncovered the truth and deploys heavily armed corporate security drones to intercept and destroy Jax's mining unit.
Jax engages in a tense zero-g dogfight, using his industrial mining lasers to fend off the sleek security drones, taking severe structural damage in the process.
A security drone scores a critical hit on Jax's thrusters, leaving him adrift in space, while back on Earth, the Warden bumps up Jax's execution time to silence him permanently.
Jax's physical body convulses as the lethal injection sequence begins, his drone systems failing rapidly, and all hope of transmitting the data seemingly gone.
Rix sacrifices his own drone to ram the remaining security vessel, giving Jax a narrow window of safety and transferring his remaining battery core to Jax.
Jax reroutes all life support and engine power from the drone to the communication array, desperately aiming his transmitter at a passing rogue satellite.
As the lethal chemicals enter Jax's veins on Earth, the drone successfully uploads the decrypted black box data to the global network, exposing the conspiracy.
The execution is violently halted by federal marshals seconds before Jax's heart stops; he awakens in a hospital bed, exonerated, breathing free air.
